structured_payload_candidate.title: Generic Zone Setpoint Separation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Each zone shall have separate occupied and unoccupied heating and cooling setpoints.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ensure four separate setpoints exist: occupied heating, occupied cooling, unoccupied heating, unoccupied cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify that each zone has all four setpoints configured.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Each zone shall have separate occupied and unoccupied heating and cooling setpoint s.

evidence_quote: Each zone shall have separate occupied and unoccupied heating and cooling setpoint s.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Setpoint Selection Based on Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active heating and cooling setpoints are set to occupied setpoints during Occupied, Warm-up, and Cooldown modes, and to unoccupied setpoints during Unoccupied, Setback, and Setup modes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply occupied setpoints in occupied/warmup/cooldown; apply unoccupied setpoints in unoccupied/setback/setup.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The active setpoint s shall be determined by the operating mode of the Zone Group (see Section 5.4.6).
a. The set points shall be the occupied set points during occupied mode, warm-up mode , and cooldown mode .
b. The set points shall be the unoccupied set points during unoccupied mode, setback mode , and setup mode .

evidence_quote:
The active setpoint s shall be determined by the operating mode of the Zone Group (see Section 5.4.6).
a. The set points shall be the occupied set points during occupied mode, warm-up mode , and cooldown mode .
b. The set points shall be the unoccupied set points during unoccupied mode, setback mode , and setup mode .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Setpoint Overlap and Limit Interlocks
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Software prevents invalid setpoint relationships: heating SP ≤ cooling SP − 0.5°C (1°F); unoccupied heating ≤ occupied heating; unoccupied cooling ≥ occupied cooling.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any attempt to set a setpoint that violates these rules
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reject or limit the setpoint to enforce relationships.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The software shall prevent the following:
a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoint s shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).
b. The unoccupied heating setpoint from exceeding the occupied heating setpoint .
c. The unoccupied cooling setpoint from being less than the occupied cooling setpoint .

evidence_quote:
The software shall prevent the following:
a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoint s shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).
b. The unoccupied heating setpoint from exceeding the occupied heating setpoint .
c. The unoccupied cooling setpoint from being less than the occupied cooling setpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Default Local Setpoint Adjustment Limits
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Default absolute limits for active occupied cooling setpoint: 22–27°C (72–80°F); active occupied heating setpoint: 18–22°C (65–72°F).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Unless overridden, the active occupied cooling setpoint shall not exceed 27°C (80°F) or go below 22°C (72°F); active occupied heating setpoint shall not exceed 22°C (72°F) or go below 18°C (65°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
1. As a default, the active occupied cooling setpoint  shall be lim ited between 22°C (72°F) and 27°C (80°F).
2. As a default, the active occupied heating setpoint  shall be limited between 18°C (65°F) and 22°C (72°F).

evidence_quote:
1. As a default, the active occupied cooling setpoint  shall be lim ited between 22°C (72°F) and 27°C (80°F).
2. As a default, the active occupied heating setpoint  shall be limited between 18°C (65°F) and 22°C (72°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Single Setpoint Adjustment Thermostat Behavior
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point adjustment, then the adjustment shall move both the active heating and cooling setpoints upward or downward by the same amount, within limits.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Simultaneously adjust active heating and cooling setpoints by the same offset, subject to limits in 5.3.2.5.b and anti-overlap logic.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point  adjustment, then the adjustment shall move both the active heating and cooling setpoint s  upward  or downward  by the same amount, within the limits described in Section 5.3.2.5.b .

evidence_quote: If zone thermostat provides only a single set-point  adjustment, then the adjustment shall move both the active heating and cooling setpoint s  upward  or downward  by the same amount, within the limits described in Section 5.3.2.5.b .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Local Setpoint Adjustment Mode Restriction
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoints in Occupied, Warmup, and Cooldown modes and have no impact in all other modes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone group mode is not Occupied, Warmup, or Cooldown
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Ignore local occupant setpoint adjustment; only affect occupied setpoints in those three modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoint s in Occupied Mode , Warmup Mode , and Cooldown Mode  and shall have no impact on setpoint s in all other modes.

evidence_quote: The adjustment shall only affect occupied setpoint s in Occupied Mode , Warmup Mode , and Cooldown Mode  and shall have no impact on setpoint s in all other modes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Demand Limiting Freezes Local Setpoint Adjustment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: At onset of demand limiting, local set-point adjustment value freezes; further adjustment suspended for duration of demand-limit event.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Freeze current local setpoint offset and ignore further occupant adjustments until demand-limit event ends.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: At the onset of demand limiting,  the local set-point  adjustment value shall be frozen. Further adjustment of the setpoint  by local controls shall be suspended for the duration of the demand -limit event.

evidence_quote: At the onset of demand limiting,  the local set-point  adjustment value shall be frozen. Further adjustment of the setpoint  by local controls shall be suspended for the duration of the demand -limit event.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Demand-Limit Setpoint Increase
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a demand limit is imposed, increase active cooling setpoints: Level 1 +0.5°C (1°F), Level 2 +1°C (2°F), Level 3 +2°C (4°F). Not cumulative; operators can exempt zones.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Demand-limit level imposed on Zone Group
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Add the corresponding increment to the active cooling setpoint, but only if not exempted.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.3.2.6.  Cooling Demand Limit Set-Point  Adjustment . The active cooling setpoint s for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group . ... a. At demand -limit  Level 1, increase setpoint  by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand -limit  Level 2,  increase setpoint  by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand -limit  Level 3, increase setpoint  by 2°C (4°F).

evidence_quote: 5.3.2.6.  Cooling Demand Limit Set-Point  Adjustment . The active cooling setpoint s for all zones shall be increased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group . ... a. At demand -limit  Level 1, increase setpoint  by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand -limit  Level 2,  increase setpoint  by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand -limit  Level 3, increase setpoint  by 2°C (4°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Demand-Limit Setpoint Decrease
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When a demand limit is imposed, decrease active heating setpoints: Level 1 −0.5°C (1°F), Level 2 −1°C (2°F), Level 3 −2°C (4°F). Not cumulative; operators can exempt zones.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Demand-limit level imposed on Zone Group
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Subtract the corresponding decrement from the active heating setpoint, unless exempted.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.3.2.7.  Heating Demand -Limit Set-Point  Adjustment . The active heating setpoint s for all zones shall be decreased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group . ... a. At demand -limit  Level 1, decrease setpoint  by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand -limit  Level 2, decrease setpoint  by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand -limit  Level 3, decrease setpoint  by 2°C (4°F).

evidence_quote: 5.3.2.7.  Heating Demand -Limit Set-Point  Adjustment . The active heating setpoint s for all zones shall be decreased when a demand limit is imposed on the associated Zone Group . ... a. At demand -limit  Level 1, decrease setpoint  by 0.5°C (1°F). b. At demand -limit  Level 2, decrease setpoint  by 1°C (2°F). c. At demand -limit  Level 3, decrease setpoint  by 2°C (4°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Window Open Setpoint Override
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: zone with operable window and switch
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When window switch indicates window open, temporarily set heating setpoint to 4°C (40°F) and cooling setpoint to 49°C (120°F).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set heating setpoint to 4°C (40°F) and cooling setpoint to 49°C (120°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: when the window switch indicates the window is open, the heating setpoint  shall be temporarily set to 4° C (40°F) and the cooling setpoint  shall be temporarily set to 49°C (120°F).

evidence_quote: when the window switch indicates the window is open, the heating setpoint  shall be temporarily set to 4° C (40°F) and the cooling setpoint  shall be temporarily set to 49°C (120°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Window Open During Non-Occupied Mode Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If window switch indicates open during any mode other than Occupied, generate a Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Window switch indicates open AND Zone Group mode is not Occupied
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the window switch indicates that the window is open during other than Occupied Mode , a Level 4 alarm shall be generated.

evidence_quote: When the window switch indicates that the window is open during other than Occupied Mode , a Level 4 alarm shall be generated.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupancy-Based Setpoint Setback
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If space unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously during Occupied Mode, decrease active heating setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase active cooling setpoint by 0.5°C (1°F).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Occupancy sensor indicates unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously while Zone Group is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously during the Occupied Mode , the active heating setpoint  shall be decreased by 0.5°C (1°F) and the cooling setpoint  shall be increased by 0.5°C (1°F).

evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been unpopulated for 5 minutes continuously during the Occupied Mode , the active heating setpoint  shall be decreased by 0.5°C (1°F) and the cooling setpoint  shall be increased by 0.5°C (1°F).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Occupancy Sensor Setpoint Restoration
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the occupancy sensor indicates the space has been populated for 1 minute continuously, restore the active heating and cooling setpoints to their previous values.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Occupancy sensor indicates populated for 1 minute continuously
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Restore setpoints to values before setback.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been populated for 1 minute continuously, the active heating and cooling setpoint s  shall be restored to their previous values.

evidence_quote: When the switch indicates that the space has been populated for 1 minute continuously, the active heating and cooling setpoint s  shall be restored to their previous values.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Setpoint Adjustment Hierarchy
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Priority order for setpoint adjustments: (1) overlap restriction, (2) absolute local limits, (3) window switches, (4) demand limit (with occupancy sensor added to demand limit and local adjustment frozen), (5) scheduled setpoints per Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Apply adjustments in specified order; demand limit overrides occupancy sensor (additive), local adjustment frozen during demand limit; overlap restriction highest priority.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.3.2.10.  Hierarchy of Set-Point  Adjustments . The foll owing adjustment restrictions shall prevail in order from highest to lowest priority:
a. Setpoint  overlap restriction (Section 5.3.2.3.a )
b. Absolute limits on local setpoint adjustment (Section 5.3.2.5.b )
c. Window switches
d. Demand limit
1. Occupancy sensors . Change of setpoint  by occupancy sensor is added to change of setpoint  by any dema nd limits in effect.
2. Local set-point  adjustment . Any changes to setpoint  by local adjustment are frozen at the onset of the demand limiting event and remain fixed for the duration of the event. Additional local adjustments are ignored for the duration of t he demand limiting event.
e. Scheduled setpoint s based on Zone Group  mode

evidence_quote:
5.3.2.10.  Hierarchy of Set-Point  Adjustments . The foll owing adjustment restrictions shall prevail in order from highest to lowest priority:
a. Setpoint  overlap restriction (Section 5.3.2.3.a )
b. Absolute limits on local setpoint adjustment (Section 5.3.2.5.b )
c. Window switches
d. Demand limit
1. Occupancy sensors . Change of setpoint  by occupancy sensor is added to change of setpoint  by any dema nd limits in effect.
2. Local set-point  adjustment . Any changes to setpoint  by local adjustment are frozen at the onset of the demand limiting event and remain fixed for the duration of the event. Additional local adjustments are ignored for the duration of t he demand limiting event.
e. Scheduled setpoint s based on Zone Group  mode

structured_payload_candidate.title: Thermostat Override Button Calls Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When thermostat override buttons are depressed, send a call for Occupied Mode operation to the Zone Group control for 60 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Transmit override signal to Zone Group, causing all zones in group to enter Occupied Mode for 60 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When thermostat override buttons are depressed, the call for Occupied Mode  operation shall be sent to the Zone Group  control for 60 minutes.

evidence_quote: When thermostat override buttons are depressed, the call for Occupied Mode  operation shall be sent to the Zone Group  control for 60 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The Heating Loop is enabled when space temperature is below the zone heating setpoint; disabled when space temperature is above the zone heating setpoint and loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if integral windup is minimized.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Space temperature relative to heating setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable when temp < heating SP; disable when temp > heating SP and output zero for 30 s.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Heating Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point  temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoi nt temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.

evidence_quote: The Heating Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point  temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoi nt temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Loop Enable/Disable
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The Cooling Loop is enabled when space temperature is above the zone cooling setpoint; disabled when space temperature is below the zone cooling setpoint and loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if integral windup is minimized.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Space temperature relative to cooling setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable when temp > cooling SP; disable when temp < cooling SP and output zero for 30 s.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone cooling set-point  temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.

evidence_quote: The Cooling Loop  shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is above the current zone cooling set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is below the current zone cooling set-point  temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds. The loop may remain active at all times if provisions are made to minimize integral windup.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Loop Control Type Requirement
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic (or equivalent); proportional-only not acceptable. P and I gains shall be adjustable by operator.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Implement PI control; integral gain small relative to proportional.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic or other technology with similar performance. Proportional -only control is not acceptable, although the integral gain shall be small relative to the proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by the operator.

evidence_quote: Loops shall use proportional + integral logic or other technology with similar performance. Proportional -only control is not acceptable, although the integral gain shall be small relative to the proportional gain. P and I gains shall be adjustable by the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone State Determination (Heating/Cooling/Deadband)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone state is Heating if Heating Loop output >0 and Cooling Loop output =0; Cooling if Cooling Loop output >0 and Heating Loop output =0; Deadband otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.3.5.1.  Heating . When  the output of the space Heating Loop is nonzero and the output of the Cooling Loop is equal to zero.
5.3.5.2.  Cooling . When  the output of the space Cooling Loop is nonzero and the output of the Heating Loop is equal to zero.
5.3.5.3.  Deadband . When  not in either heating  or cooling .

evidence_quote:
5.3.5.1.  Heating . When  the output of the space Heating Loop is nonzero and the output of the Cooling Loop is equal to zero.
5.3.5.2.  Cooling . When  the output of the space Cooling Loop is nonzero and the output of the Heating Loop is equal to zero.
5.3.5.3.  Deadband . When  not in either heating  or cooling .

structured_payload_candidate.title: High Temperature Level 4 Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temp > cooling setpoint + 2°C (3°F) for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: High Temperature Level 3 Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temp > cooling setpoint + 3°C (5°F) for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the zone is 3°C (5°F) above cooling setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the zone is 3°C (5°F) above cooling setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Temperature Level 4 Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if zone temperature is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temp < heating setpoint - 2°C (3°F) for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Temperature Level 3 Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if zone temperature is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temp < heating setpoint - 3°C (5°F) for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint  for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Temperature Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress zone temperature alarms after a zone setpoint change (per 5.1.20) and while Zone Group is in Warmup or Cooldown Mode. Do not suppress in setup, setback, or Unoccupied Modes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Alarm condition exists but suppression criteria met
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Prevent alarm generation during setpoint change transient and during warmup/cooldown; allow alarms in other modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
c. Suppress zone temperature alarms as follows:
1. After zone setpoi nt is changed per Section 5.1.20 .
2. While Zone Group  is in Warmup  Mode or Cooldown Mode .

evidence_quote:
c. Suppress zone temperature alarms as follows:
1. After zone setpoi nt is changed per Section 5.1.20 .
2. While Zone Group  is in Warmup  Mode or Cooldown Mode .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Consider Shorter Zone Temperature Alarm Delay for Critical Zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The default 10-minute alarm delay may be too long for critical zones (e.g., IT closets); consider reducing or eliminating the delay.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For critical zones , such as IT closets, consider reducing time delay or setting delay to zero.

evidence_quote: For critical zones , such as IT closets, consider reducing time delay or setting delay to zero.
